<content>
package main

import (
	"crypto/rand"
	"database/sql"
	"encoding/hex"
	"fmt"
	"log"
	"net/http"
	"os"
	"strings"
	"time"

	"github.com/gin-contrib/cors"
	"github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
	"github.com/golang-jwt/jwt/v5"
	_ "github.com/lib/pq"
	"golang.org/x/crypto/bcrypt"
)

var (
	db        *sql.DB
	jwtSecret []byte
)

type User struct {
	ID       int    `json:"id"`
	Name     string `json:"name"`
	Email    string `json:"email"`
	Password string `json:"-"`
	Role     string `json:"role"`
	TenantID int    `json:"tenantId"`
	Status   string `json:"status"`
}

type Claims struct {
	UserID   int    `json:"userId"`
	Email    string `json:"email"`
	Role     string `json:"role"`
	TenantID int    `json:"tenantId"`
	jwt.RegisteredClaims
}

func init() {
	secret := os.Getenv("JWT_SECRET")
	if secret == "" {
		// Generate random secret if not provided (dev mode)
		b := make([]byte, 32)
		rand.Read(b)
		secret = hex.EncodeToString(b)
		log.Println("[AUTH] WARNING: Using random JWT secret. Set JWT_SECRET env var for production.")
	}
	jwtSecret = []byte(secret)
}

func initDB() {
	connStr := os.Getenv("DB_URL")
	if connStr == "" {
		connStr = "postgres://audira:password@localhost:5432/audira_grid?sslmode=disable"
	}

	var err error
	db, err = sql.Open("postgres", connStr)
	if err != nil {
		log.Fatalf("[AUTH DB] Connection failed: %v", err)
	}

	db.SetMaxOpenConns(25)
	db.SetMaxIdleConns(5)
	db.SetConnMaxLifetime(5 * time.Minute)

	// Test connection
	if err = db.Ping(); err != nil {
		log.Printf("[AUTH DB] Ping failed: %v", err)
		log.Println("[AUTH DB] Will retry in 3 seconds...")
		time.Sleep(3 * time.Second)
		if err = db.Ping(); err != nil {
			log.Printf("[AUTH DB] Retry failed: %v — running without DB", err)
			db = nil
			return
		}
	}
	log.Println("[AUTH DB] Connected successfully")

	// Create users table
	_, err = db.Exec(`
		CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS users (
			id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
			name TEXT NOT NULL,
			email TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL,
			password_hash TEXT NOT NULL,
			role TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'Viewer',
			tenant_id INTEGER DEFAULT 1,
			status TEXT DEFAULT 'Active',
			totp_secret TEXT,
			totp_enabled BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE,
			last_login TIMESTAMP,
			created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
			updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
		);
		CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS refresh_tokens (
			id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
			user_id INTEGER REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
			token TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL,
			expires_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,
			created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
		);
		CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS backup_codes (
			id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
			user_id INTEGER REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
			code TEXT NOT NULL,
			used BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE,
			used_at TIMESTAMP,
			UNIQUE(user_id, code)
		);
	`)
	if err != nil {
		log.Printf("[AUTH DB] Migration error: %v", err)
	}

	// Seed default admin user if none exists
	var count int
	db.QueryRow("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM users").Scan(&count)
	if count == 0 {
		hash, _ := bcrypt.GenerateFromPassword([]byte("admin123"), bcrypt.DefaultCost)
		db.Exec(
			"INSERT INTO users (name, email, password_hash, role, tenant_id) VALUES ($1, $2, $3, $4, $5)",
			"Admin", "admin@audira.com", string(hash), "Administrator", 1,
		)
		log.Println("[AUTH] Default admin user created: admin@audira.com / admin123")
	}

	log.Println("[AUTH DB] Initialized successfully")
}

func generateToken(user User) (string, error) {
	claims := Claims{
		UserID:   user.ID,
		Email:    user.Email,
		Role:     user.Role,
		TenantID: user.TenantID,
		RegisteredClaims: jwt.RegisteredClaims{
			ExpiresAt: jwt.NewNumericDate(time.Now().Add(24 * time.Hour)),
			IssuedAt:  jwt.NewNumericDate(time.Now()),
			Issuer:    "audira-auth",
		},
	}

	token := jwt.NewWithClaims(jwt.SigningMethodHS256, claims)
	return token.SignedString(jwtSecret)
}

func generateRefreshToken(userID int) (string, error) {
	b := make([]byte, 32)
	rand.Read(b)
	token := hex.EncodeToString(b)

	expiresAt := time.Now().Add(7 * 24 * time.Hour)
	_, err := db.Exec(
		"INSERT INTO refresh_tokens (user_id, token, expires_at) VALUES ($1, $2, $3)",
		userID, token, expiresAt,
	)
	return token, err
}

// AuthMiddleware validates JWT tokens
func AuthMiddleware() gin.HandlerFunc {
	return func(c *gin.Context) {
		authHeader := c.GetHeader("Authorization")
		if authHeader == "" {
			c.AbortWithStatusJSON(http.StatusUnauthorized, gin.H{"error": "Authorization header required"})
			return
		}

		parts := strings.SplitN(authHeader, " ", 2)
		if len(parts) != 2 || parts[0] != "Bearer" {
			c.AbortWithStatusJSON(http.StatusUnauthorized, gin.H{"error": "Invalid authorization format"})
			return
		}

		token, err := jwt.ParseWithClaims(parts[1], &Claims{}, func(token *jwt.Token) (interface{}, error) {
			if _, ok := token.Method.(*jwt.SigningMethodHMAC); !ok {
				return nil, fmt.Errorf("unexpected signing method: %v", token.Header["alg"])
			}
			return jwtSecret, nil
		})

		if err != nil || !token.Valid {
			c.AbortWithStatusJSON(http.StatusUnauthorized, gin.H{"error": "Invalid or expired token"})
			return
		}

		claims, ok := token.Claims.(*Claims)
		if !ok {
			c.AbortWithStatusJSON(http.StatusUnauthorized, gin.H{"error": "Invalid token claims"})
			return
		}

		c.Set("userId", claims.UserID)
		c.Set("email", claims.Email)
		c.Set("role", claims.Role)
		c.Set("tenantId", claims.TenantID)
		c.Next()
	}
}

// RoleMiddleware checks if user has required role
func RoleMiddleware(allowedRoles ...string) gin.HandlerFunc {
	return func(c *gin.Context) {
		role, exists := c.Get("role")
		if !exists {
			c.AbortWithStatusJSON(http.StatusForbidden, gin.H{"error": "No role found"})
			return
		}

		userRole := role.(string)
		for _, allowed := range allowedRoles {
			if userRole == allowed {
				c.Next()
				return
			}
		}

		c.AbortWithStatusJSON(http.StatusForbidden, gin.H{"error": "Insufficient permissions"})
	}
}

func main() {
	initDB()
	gin.SetMode(gin.ReleaseMode)

	r := gin.New()
	r.Use(gin.Recovery())

	// CORS - configurable via env
	allowedOrigins := os.Getenv("CORS_ORIGINS")
	if allowedOrigins == "" {
		allowedOrigins = "http://localhost:1420,http://localhost:1421,http://localhost:5173"
	}

	r.Use(cors.New(cors.Config{
		AllowOrigins:     strings.Split(allowedOrigins, ","),
		AllowMethods:     []string{"GET", "POST", "PUT", "DELETE", "OPTIONS"},
		AllowHeaders:     []string{"Content-Type", "Authorization"},
		AllowCredentials: true,
		MaxAge:           12 * time.Hour,
	}))

	// Health check
	r.GET("/health", func(c *gin.Context) {
		c.JSON(http.StatusOK, gin.H{"status": "UP", "service": "auth-service"})
	})

	// Public routes
	r.POST("/api/v1/auth/login", func(c *gin.Context) {
		var input struct {
			Email    string `json:"email" binding:"required"`
			Password string `json:"password" binding:"required"`
		}
		if err := c.ShouldBindJSON(&input); err != nil {
			c.JSON(http.StatusBadRequest, gin.H{"error": "Email and password are required"})
			return
		}

		var user User
		var passwordHash string
		err := db.QueryRow(
			"SELECT id, name, email, password_hash, role, tenant_id, status FROM users WHERE email = $1",
			input.Email,
		).Scan(&user.ID, &user.Name, &user.Email, &passwordHash, &user.Role, &user.TenantID, &user.Status)

		if err != nil {
			c.JSON(http.StatusUnauthorized, gin.H{"error": "Invalid credentials"})
			return
		}

		if user.Status != "Active" {
			c.JSON(http.StatusForbidden, gin.H{"error": "Account is disabled"})
			return
		}

		if err := bcrypt.CompareHashAndPassword([]byte(passwordHash), []byte(input.Password)); err != nil {
			c.JSON(http.StatusUnauthorized, gin.H{"error": "Invalid credentials"})
			return
		}

		token, err := generateToken(user)
		if err != nil {
			c.JSON(http.StatusInternalServerError, gin.H{"error": "Failed to generate token"})
			return
		}

		refreshToken, _ := generateRefreshToken(user.ID)

		// Update last login
		db.Exec("UPDATE users SET last_login = $1 WHERE id = $2", time.Now(), user.ID)

		c.JSON(http.StatusOK, gin.H{
			"token":        token,
			"refreshToken": refreshToken,
			"user": gin.H{
				"id":    user.ID,
				"name":  user.Name,
				"email": user.Email,
				"role":  user.Role,
			},
		})
	})

	r.POST("/api/v1/auth/refresh", func(c *gin.Context) {
		var input struct {
			RefreshToken string `json:"refreshToken" binding:"required"`
		}
		if err := c.ShouldBindJSON(&input); err != nil {
			c.JSON(http.StatusBadRequest, gin.H{"error": "Refresh token required"})
			return
		}

		var userID int
		var expiresAt time.Time
		err := db.QueryRow(
			"SELECT user_id, expires_at FROM refresh_tokens WHERE token = $1",
			input.RefreshToken,
		).Scan(&userID, &expiresAt)

		if err != nil || time.Now().After(expiresAt) {
			c.JSON(http.StatusUnauthorized, gin.H{"error": "Invalid or expired refresh token"})
			return
		}

		var user User
		db.QueryRow(
			"SELECT id, name, email, role, tenant_id FROM users WHERE id = $1",
			userID,
		).Scan(&user.ID, &user.Name, &user.Email, &user.Role, &user.TenantID)

		token, _ := generateToken(user)

		// Rotate refresh token
		db.Exec("DELETE FROM refresh_tokens WHERE token = $1", input.RefreshToken)
		newRefresh, _ := generateRefreshToken(userID)

		c.JSON(http.StatusOK, gin.H{
			"token":        token,
			"refreshToken": newRefresh,
		})
	})

	// Protected routes
	protected := r.Group("/api/v1")
	protected.Use(AuthMiddleware())

	protected.GET("/auth/me", func(c *gin.Context) {
		userID, _ := c.Get("userId")
		var user User
		db.QueryRow(
			"SELECT id, name, email, role, tenant_id, status FROM users WHERE id = $1",
			userID,
		).Scan(&user.ID, &user.Name, &user.Email, &user.Role, &user.TenantID, &user.Status)
		c.JSON(http.StatusOK, user)
	})

	protected.POST("/auth/logout", func(c *gin.Context) {
		userID, _ := c.Get("userId")
		db.Exec("DELETE FROM refresh_tokens WHERE user_id = $1", userID)
		c.JSON(http.StatusOK, gin.H{"message": "Logged out"})
	})

	// 2FA routes (protected)
	Register2FARoutes(protected.Group(""))

	// User management (admin only)
	admin := protected.Group("")
	admin.Use(RoleMiddleware("Administrator", "Super Administrator"))

	admin.GET("/users", func(c *gin.Context) {
		rows, err := db.Query("SELECT id, name, email, role, tenant_id, status, created_at FROM users ORDER BY created_at DESC")
		if err != nil {
			c.JSON(http.StatusInternalServerError, gin.H{"error": err.Error()})
			return
		}
		defer rows.Close()

		var users []map[string]interface{}
		for rows.Next() {
			var id, tenantID int
			var name, email, role, status string
			var createdAt time.Time
			rows.Scan(&id, &name, &email, &role, &tenantID, &status, &createdAt)
			users = append(users, map[string]interface{}{
				"id": id, "name": name, "email": email, "role": role,
				"tenantId": tenantID, "status": status, "createdAt": createdAt,
			})
		}
		c.JSON(http.StatusOK, users)
	})

	admin.POST("/users", func(c *gin.Context) {
		var input struct {
			Name     string `json:"name" binding:"required"`
			Email    string `json:"email" binding:"required"`
			Password string `json:"password" binding:"required"`
			Role     string `json:"role"`
		}
		if err := c.ShouldBindJSON(&input); err != nil {
			c.JSON(http.StatusBadRequest, gin.H{"error": "Name, email, and password are required"})
			return
		}
		if input.Role == "" {
			input.Role = "Viewer"
		}

		hash, _ := bcrypt.GenerateFromPassword([]byte(input.Password), bcrypt.DefaultCost)
		tenantID, _ := c.Get("tenantId")

		var id int
		err := db.QueryRow(
			"INSERT INTO users (name, email, password_hash, role, tenant_id) VALUES ($1, $2, $3, $4, $5) RETURNING id",
			input.Name, input.Email, string(hash), input.Role, tenantID,
		).Scan(&id)

		if err != nil {
			c.JSON(http.StatusConflict, gin.H{"error": "User with this email already exists"})
			return
		}

		c.JSON(http.StatusCreated, gin.H{"id": id, "message": "User created"})
	})

	admin.DELETE("/users/:id", func(c *gin.Context) {
		id := c.Param("id")
		db.Exec("DELETE FROM users WHERE id = $1", id)
		c.JSON(http.StatusOK, gin.H{"message": "User deleted"})
	})

	// Token validation endpoint (for other services)
	r.POST("/api/v1/auth/validate", func(c *gin.Context) {
		var input struct {
			Token string `json:"token" binding:"required"`
		}
		if err := c.ShouldBindJSON(&input); err != nil {
			c.JSON(http.StatusBadRequest, gin.H{"error": "Token required"})
			return
		}

		token, err := jwt.ParseWithClaims(input.Token, &Claims{}, func(token *jwt.Token) (interface{}, error) {
			return jwtSecret, nil
		})

		if err != nil || !token.Valid {
			c.JSON(http.StatusUnauthorized, gin.H{"valid": false})
			return
		}

		claims := token.Claims.(*Claims)
		c.JSON(http.StatusOK, gin.H{
			"valid":    true,
			"userId":   claims.UserID,
			"email":    claims.Email,
			"role":     claims.Role,
			"tenantId": claims.TenantID,
		})
	})

	port := os.Getenv("PORT")
	if port == "" {
		port = "8080"
	}

	log.Printf("[AUTH] Service starting on :%s", port)
	if err := r.Run(":" + port); err != nil {
		log.Fatalf("Failed to start auth service: %v", err)
	}
}

import { AIVisionPage } from './pages/AIVisionPage';
import { BillingPage } from './pages/BillingPage';
import { ApiKeysPage } from './pages/ApiKeysPage';
import { TenantsPage } from './pages/TenantsPage';
import { UploadCenterPage } from './pages/UploadCenterPage';
import { NotificationsPage } from './pages/NotificationsPage';
import { PluginStorePage } from './pages/PluginStorePage';
import { AutomationManager } from './pages/AutomationManager';
import { ProxyManager } from './pages/ProxyManager';
import { HealthMonitor } from './pages/HealthMonitor';
import { AppRepository } from './pages/AppRepository';
import { ThemeProvider } from './hooks/useTheme';
import { ErrorBoundary } from './components/ErrorBoundary';
import { ToastContainer } from './components/ToastContainer';
import { OnboardingWizard } from './components/OnboardingWizard';
import { AIAssistant } from './components/AIAssistant';
import { SplashScreen } from './components/SplashScreen';
import { useAuthStore, useAppStore } from './store';
import { useDeviceDetection } from './hooks/useDeviceDetection';
import { useKeyboardShortcuts } from './hooks/useKeyboardShortcuts';

const ComingSoon = ({ title }: { title: string }) => (
  <div className="flex-1 flex flex-col items-center justify-center p-12">
    <div className="text-center">
      <div className="inline-block px-4 py-1.5 mb-4 text-[10px] font-black tracking-widest uppercase" style={{ border: '2px solid var(--primary)', color: 'var(--primary)' }}>
        Coming Soon
      </div>
      <h2 className="text-4xl font-black tracking-tighter mb-2" style={{ color: 'var(--foreground)' }}>{title}</h2>
      <p className="text-sm" style={{ color: 'var(--muted)' }}>This module is under active development.</p>
    </div>
  </div>
);

function AppContent() {
  const { isAuthenticated, logout } = useAuthStore();
  const { currentPage, navigate, isCommandPaletteOpen, setCommandPaletteOpen, toggleCommandPalette, orientation, setOrientation } = useAppStore();
  const [selectedDeviceId, setSelectedDeviceId] = React.useState<string | null>(null);
  const [showOnboarding, setShowOnboarding] = React.useState(false);

  // Auto-detect devices and show notifications
  useDeviceDetection(isAuthenticated);

  // Global keyboard shortcuts
  useKeyboardShortcuts();

  // Check if first time user
  useEffect(() => {
    if (isAuthenticated && !localStorage.getItem('audira-onboarded')) {
      setShowOnboarding(true);
    }
  }, [isAuthenticated]);

  useEffect(() => {
    const handleKeyDown = (e: KeyboardEvent) => {
      if ((e.metaKey || e.ctrlKey) && e.key === 'k') {
        e.preventDefault();
        toggleCommandPalette();
      }
    };

    // Listen for auth expiry
    const handleAuthExpired = () => {
      logout();
    };
    window.addEventListener('auth:expired', handleAuthExpired);
    window.addEventListener('keydown', handleKeyDown);
    return () => {
      window.removeEventListener('keydown', handleKeyDown);
      window.removeEventListener('auth:expired', handleAuthExpired);
    };
  }, [toggleCommandPalette, logout]);

  if (!isAuthenticated) {
    return <LoginPage onLogin={() => {}} />;
  }

  if (showOnboarding) {
    return (
      <OnboardingWizard
        onComplete={() => { localStorage.setItem('audira-onboarded', 'true'); setShowOnboarding(false); }}
        onSkip={() => { localStorage.setItem('audira-onboarded', 'true'); setShowOnboarding(false); }}
      />
    );
  }

  const renderPage = () => {
    switch (currentPage) {
      case 'dashboard':
        return <Dashboard />;
      case 'live-devices': return <LiveDevices selectedDeviceId={selectedDeviceId} onSelectDevice={setSelectedDeviceId} />;
      case 'device-control': return <DeviceControl deviceId={selectedDeviceId || ''} orientation={orientation} setOrientation={setOrientation} onBack={() => navigate('live-devices')} />;
      case 'roles-permissions': return <RolesPermissions />;
      case 'groups': return <Groups />;
      case 'workflow-studio': return <WorkflowStudio />;
      case 'automation': return <AutomationManager />;
      case 'proxy-manager': return <ProxyManager />;
      case 'health-monitor': return <HealthMonitor />;
      case 'app-repo': return <AppRepository />;
      case 'analytics': return <AnalyticsPage />;
      case 'logs': return <LogsPage />;
      case 'notifications': return <NotificationsPage />;
      case 'settings': return <SettingsPage />;
      case 'users': case 'roles': return <UsersPage />;
      case 'ai-vision': return <AIVisionPage />;
      case 'billing': return <BillingPage />;
      case 'api-keys': return <ApiKeysPage />;
      case 'tenants': return <TenantsPage />;
      case 'uploads': return <UploadCenterPage />;
      case 'plugins': return <PluginStorePage />;
      default: return <ComingSoon title={currentPage} />;
    }
  };

  const renderRightPanel = () => {
    switch (currentPage) {
      case 'live-devices': return <DeviceDetailSidebar deviceId={selectedDeviceId} onInteractiveMode={() => navigate('device-control')} />;
      case 'device-control': return <ControlSidebarRight deviceId={selectedDeviceId || ''} orientation={orientation} setOrientation={setOrientation} />;
      case 'groups': return <GroupsSidebarRight />;
      default: return null;
    }
  };

  return (
    <div className="flex h-screen overflow-hidden" style={{ background: 'var(--background)' }}>
      <Sidebar onNavigate={navigate} currentPage={currentPage} />
      <div className="flex-1 flex flex-col min-w-0">
        <Header />
        <div className="flex-1 flex overflow-hidden">
          <div className="flex-1 flex flex-col overflow-hidden">
            {renderPage()}
          </div>
          {renderRightPanel()}
        </div>
      </div>
      
      <CommandPalette 
        isOpen={isCommandPaletteOpen} 
        onClose={() => setCommandPaletteOpen(false)} 
        onNavigate={navigate} 
      />
      <AIAssistant deviceId={selectedDeviceId} />
    </div>
  );
}

function App() {
  const [showSplash, setShowSplash] = React.useState(true);

  if (showSplash) {
    return <SplashScreen onComplete={() => setShowSplash(false)} />;
  }

  return (
    <ThemeProvider>
      <ErrorBoundary>
        <AppContent />
        <ToastContainer />
      </ErrorBoundary>
    </ThemeProvider>
  );
}

export default App;


</content>
</file>

<file name="./docs/specs/device-performance-monitoring/requirements.md" language="markdown" >
<content>
# Feature Requirements: Device Performance Monitoring

**Feature ID:** DPM-001  
**Priority:** High  
**Status:** Draft  
**Created:** 2026-05-16  
**Owner:** Engineering Team

---

## 1. Overview

### 1.1 Feature Summary
Real-time and historical monitoring of Android device performance metrics including CPU usage, RAM usage, battery level, and temperature. The system will provide visualization, alerting, reporting, and multi-device comparison capabilities.

### 1.2 Business Goals
- Enable proactive device health monitoring to prevent test failures
- Provide performance insights for device farm optimization
- Support capacity planning through historical data analysis
- Reduce device downtime through early warning alerts
- Enable performance comparison across device models and configurations

### 1.3 Success Metrics
- Performance data latency < 2 seconds from device to UI
- Support monitoring of 100+ concurrent devices
- Alert delivery within 5 seconds of threshold breach
- Historical data retention for 90 days minimum
- 99.9% uptime for monitoring service

---

## 2. Functional Requirements

### 2.1 Real-Time Performance Monitoring

#### 2.1.1 Metrics Collection
**REQ-DPM-001:** The system SHALL collect the following metrics from each connected device:
- CPU usage (percentage, per-core breakdown)
- RAM usage (used/total MB, percentage)
- Battery level (percentage)
- Battery temperature (Celsius)
- Device temperature (Celsius, if available)

**REQ-DPM-002:** Metrics SHALL be collected at configurable intervals with a default of 1 second and maximum of 10 seconds.

**REQ-DPM-003:** The system SHALL use ADB commands for local devices:
- `adb shell dumpsys cpuinfo` for CPU metrics
- `adb shell dumpsys meminfo` for RAM metrics
- `adb shell dumpsys battery` for battery metrics
- `adb shell cat /sys/class/thermal/thermal_zone*/temp` for temperature

**REQ-DPM-004:** The system SHALL support both local (ADB) and cloud-connected devices with the same metric collection interface.

**REQ-DPM-005:** If metric collection fails for a device, the system SHALL retry up to 3 times with exponential backoff before marking the metric as unavailable.

#### 2.1.2 Real-Time Display
**REQ-DPM-006:** The UI SHALL display current performance metrics with visual indicators:
- Numeric values with units
- Progress bars or gauges for percentage-based metrics
- Color coding: green (normal), yellow (warning), red (critical)

**REQ-DPM-007:** The UI SHALL update performance displays within 2 seconds of metric collection.

**REQ-DPM-008:** The system SHALL use WebSocket connections for real-time metric streaming to the UI.

**REQ-DPM-009:** The UI SHALL display the last update timestamp for each metric.

**REQ-DPM-010:** If a device becomes disconnected, the UI SHALL display a "disconnected" state and stop updating metrics.

### 2.2 Historical Data Tracking

#### 2.2.1 Data Storage
**REQ-DPM-011:** The system SHALL store all collected metrics in PostgreSQL with the following schema:
- device_id (foreign key)
- metric_type (cpu, ram, battery_level, temperature)
- metric_value (numeric)
- timestamp (UTC)
- metadata (JSON for additional context)

**REQ-DPM-012:** The system SHALL implement time-series partitioning for performance metrics by day.

**REQ-DPM-013:** The system SHALL retain raw metrics for 7 days, then aggregate to 1-minute averages for 30 days, then 1-hour averages for 90 days.

**REQ-DPM-014:** The system SHALL implement automatic data cleanup for metrics older than 90 days.

**REQ-DPM-015:** The system SHALL use Redis for caching recent metrics (last 5 minutes) to reduce database load.

#### 2.2.2 Data Visualization
**REQ-DPM-016:** The UI SHALL provide time-series charts for each metric type with the following capabilities:
- Selectable time ranges: 1 hour, 6 hours, 24 hours, 7 days, 30 days
- Zoom and pan functionality
- Multi-metric overlay on single chart
- Tooltip showing exact values on hover

**REQ-DPM-017:** Charts SHALL use a charting library (e.g., Chart.js, Recharts, or Apache ECharts) optimized for time-series data.

**REQ-DPM-018:** The system SHALL support exporting chart data as CSV or JSON.

**REQ-DPM-019:** The UI SHALL display statistical summaries for selected time ranges:
- Average, minimum, maximum values
- Standard deviation
- Percentiles (50th, 95th, 99th)

### 2.3 Alert System

#### 2.3.1 Threshold Configuration
**REQ-DPM-020:** Users SHALL be able to configure alert thresholds for each metric type:
- CPU usage > X%
- RAM usage > X%
- Battery level < X%
- Temperature > X°C

**REQ-DPM-021:** Thresholds SHALL support two levels: warning and critical.

**REQ-DPM-022:** Thresholds SHALL be configurable globally (all devices) or per-device.

**REQ-DPM-023:** The system SHALL provide default threshold values:
- CPU: warning 80%, critical 95%
- RAM: warning 85%, critical 95%
- Battery: warning 20%, critical 10%
- Temperature: warning 40°C, critical 50°C

**REQ-DPM-024:** Threshold configurations SHALL be stored in PostgreSQL and cached in Redis.

#### 2.3.2 Alert Generation
**REQ-DPM-025:** The system SHALL evaluate thresholds in real-time as metrics are collected.

**REQ-DPM-026:** Alerts SHALL be generated when a metric exceeds a threshold for 3 consecutive readings to avoid false positives.

**REQ-DPM-027:** The system SHALL implement alert deduplication with a 5-minute cooldown period per device per metric.

**REQ-DPM-028:** Each alert SHALL contain:
- Device ID and name
- Metric type and current value
- Threshold value and level (warning/critical)
- Timestamp
- Alert ID (unique)

#### 2.3.3 Alert Delivery
**REQ-DPM-029:** Alerts SHALL be delivered through the following channels:
- In-app notifications (real-time via WebSocket)
- Desktop notifications (Tauri notification API)
- Alert history log (stored in database)

**REQ-DPM-030:** Users SHALL be able to acknowledge alerts to dismiss them from active view.

**REQ-DPM-031:** The system SHALL maintain an alert history for 30 days.

**REQ-DPM-032:** The UI SHALL display active alerts in a dedicated notification center with filtering by severity and device.

### 2.4 Performance Reports

#### 2.4.1 Report Generation
**REQ-DPM-033:** Users SHALL be able to generate performance reports for:
- Single device over a time range
- Multiple devices over a time range
- Device group comparisons

**REQ-DPM-034:** Reports SHALL include:
- Summary statistics for each metric
- Time-series charts
- Alert history during the period
- Device uptime/availability
- Anomaly detection highlights

**REQ-DPM-035:** The system SHALL support report export in the following formats:
- PDF (with charts and tables)
- CSV (raw data)
- JSON (structured data)

**REQ-DPM-036:** Report generation SHALL be performed asynchronously for time ranges > 24 hours.

**REQ-DPM-037:** Users SHALL receive a notification when async reports are ready for download.

#### 2.4.2 Scheduled Reports
**REQ-DPM-038:** Users SHALL be able to schedule recurring reports:
- Daily, weekly, or monthly frequency
- Configurable recipients (future: email integration)
- Automatic generation and storage

**REQ-DPM-039:** Scheduled reports SHALL be stored for 90 days before automatic deletion.

### 2.5 Multi-Device Comparison

#### 2.5.1 Comparison View
**REQ-DPM-040:** The UI SHALL provide a comparison view displaying metrics for multiple devices simultaneously.

**REQ-DPM-041:** Users SHALL be able to select 2-10 devices for comparison.

**REQ-DPM-042:** The comparison view SHALL display:
- Side-by-side current metrics
- Overlaid time-series charts
- Relative performance indicators (best/worst)
- Device specifications for context

**REQ-DPM-043:** The system SHALL support filtering devices by:
- Device model
- Android version
- Status (online/offline)
- Performance tier (based on historical averages)

**REQ-DPM-044:** Users SHALL be able to save comparison configurations as presets.

---

## 3. Non-Functional Requirements

### 3.1 Performance
**REQ-DPM-NFR-001:** Metric collection SHALL NOT impact device performance by more than 2% CPU usage.

**REQ-DPM-NFR-002:** The monitoring service SHALL support 100+ concurrent device connections.

**REQ-DPM-NFR-003:** Database queries for historical data SHALL return results within 3 seconds for 30-day ranges.

**REQ-DPM-NFR-004:** The UI SHALL render charts with 10,000+ data points without lag.

**REQ-DPM-NFR-005:** WebSocket message latency SHALL be < 100ms under normal network conditions.

### 3.2 Scalability
**REQ-DPM-NFR-006:** The system SHALL scale horizontally to support 1000+ devices across multiple monitoring service instances.

**REQ-DPM-NFR-007:** Database partitioning SHALL support efficient queries across 1TB+ of historical data.

**REQ-DPM-NFR-008:** Redis cache SHALL be configured with LRU eviction and 2GB minimum memory allocation.

### 3.3 Reliability
**REQ-DPM-NFR-009:** The monitoring service SHALL have 99.9% uptime.

**REQ-DPM-NFR-010:** Metric collection failures SHALL NOT crash the monitoring service.

**REQ-DPM-NFR-011:** The system SHALL implement circuit breakers for database and Redis connections.

**REQ-DPM-NFR-012:** All critical operations SHALL be logged for debugging and audit purposes.

### 3.4 Security
**REQ-DPM-NFR-013:** All API endpoints SHALL require authentication via JWT tokens.

**REQ-DPM-NFR-014:** WebSocket connections SHALL be authenticated before streaming metrics.

**REQ-DPM-NFR-015:** Alert configurations SHALL be scoped to user permissions (admin vs. regular user).

**REQ-DPM-NFR-016:** Exported reports SHALL NOT contain sensitive device information unless user has appropriate permissions.

### 3.5 Maintainability
**REQ-DPM-NFR-017:** The monitoring service SHALL be implemented as a separate microservice in Go.

**REQ-DPM-NFR-018:** The codebase SHALL include unit tests with 80%+ coverage.

**REQ-DPM-NFR-019:** The system SHALL expose Prometheus metrics for observability.

**REQ-DPM-NFR-020:** Configuration SHALL be externalized via environment variables and config files.

---

## 4. Integration Requirements

### 4.1 Device Service Integration
**REQ-DPM-INT-001:** The monitoring service SHALL integrate with the existing device-service via gRPC or REST API.

**REQ-DPM-INT-002:** The device-service SHALL provide device connection status events to the monitoring service.

**REQ-DPM-INT-003:** The monitoring service SHALL query device-service for device metadata (model, OS version, etc.).

### 4.2 API Gateway Integration
**REQ-DPM-INT-004:** All monitoring APIs SHALL be exposed through the existing api-gateway.

**REQ-DPM-INT-005:** The api-gateway SHALL implement rate limiting for monitoring endpoints (100 requests/minute per user).

### 4.3 Auth Service Integration
**REQ-DPM-INT-006:** The monitoring service SHALL validate JWT tokens via the auth-service.

**REQ-DPM-INT-007:** Permission checks SHALL be performed for alert configuration and report generation.

### 4.4 Database Integration
**REQ-DPM-INT-008:** The monitoring service SHALL use the existing PostgreSQL instance with a dedicated schema.

**REQ-DPM-INT-009:** Database migrations SHALL be managed via a migration tool (e.g., golang-migrate).

### 4.5 Redis Integration
**REQ-DPM-INT-010:** The monitoring service SHALL use the existing Redis instance with a dedicated key prefix.

**REQ-DPM-INT-011:** Redis keys SHALL have appropriate TTL values to prevent memory bloat.

---

## 5. User Interface Requirements

### 5.1 Dashboard Integration
**REQ-DPM-UI-001:** A new "Performance" section SHALL be added to the main navigation sidebar.

**REQ-DPM-UI-002:** The dashboard SHALL display a performance overview widget showing:
- Number of devices with active alerts
- Average CPU/RAM usage across all devices
- Devices with critical status

### 5.2 Device Detail View
**REQ-DPM-UI-003:** The existing device detail view SHALL be enhanced with a "Performance" tab.

**REQ-DPM-UI-004:** The performance tab SHALL display:
- Real-time metrics with gauges
- Historical charts (last 24 hours by default)
- Recent alerts for the device
- Quick actions (export report, configure alerts)

### 5.3 Performance Monitoring Page
**REQ-DPM-UI-005:** A dedicated performance monitoring page SHALL provide:
- Multi-device comparison view
- Advanced filtering and search
- Bulk alert configuration
- Report generation interface

### 5.4 Alert Center
**REQ-DPM-UI-006:** A notification center SHALL display:
- Active alerts (unacknowledged)
- Alert history with filtering
- Alert statistics (count by severity, device)

### 5.5 Responsive Design
**REQ-DPM-UI-007:** All performance monitoring UI components SHALL be responsive and work on desktop screens (1920x1080 minimum).

**REQ-DPM-UI-008:** Charts SHALL be interactive with touch/mouse support for zoom and pan.

---

## 6. Data Requirements

### 6.1 Database Schema

#### 6.1.1 performance_metrics Table
```sql
CREATE TABLE performance_metrics (
    id BIGSERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
    device_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES devices(id),
    metric_type VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
    metric_value NUMERIC(10, 2) NOT NULL,
    metadata JSONB,
    created_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
) PARTITION BY RANGE (created_at);
```

#### 6.1.2 alert_thresholds Table
```sql
CREATE TABLE alert_thresholds (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    device_id UUID REFERENCES devices(id), -- NULL for global
    metric_type VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
    warning_threshold NUMERIC(10, 2),
    critical_threshold NUMERIC(10, 2),
    enabled BOOLEAN DEFAULT true,
    created_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
    updated_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
);
```

#### 6.1.3 alerts Table
```sql
CREATE TABLE alerts (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    device_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES devices(id),
    metric_type VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
    severity VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL, -- 'warning' or 'critical'
    metric_value NUMERIC(10, 2) NOT NULL,
    threshold_value NUMERIC(10, 2) NOT NULL,
    acknowledged BOOLEAN DEFAULT false,
    acknowledged_at TIMESTAMP,
    acknowledged_by UUID REFERENCES users(id),
    created_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
);
```

#### 6.1.4 performance_reports Table
```sql
CREATE TABLE performance_reports (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    name VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    report_type VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL, -- 'single', 'multi', 'comparison'
    device_ids UUID[] NOT NULL,
    time_range_start TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,
    time_range_end TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,
    format VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL, -- 'pdf', 'csv', 'json'
    file_path TEXT,
    status VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL, -- 'pending', 'completed', 'failed'
    created_by UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),
    created_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
    completed_at TIMESTAMP
);
```

### 6.2 Redis Data Structures

**REQ-DPM-DATA-001:** Recent metrics cache:
- Key: `perf:metrics:{device_id}:{metric_type}`
- Type: Sorted Set (score = timestamp)
- TTL: 5 minutes

**REQ-DPM-DATA-002:** Alert cooldown tracking:
- Key: `perf:alert:cooldown:{device_id}:{metric_type}`
- Type: String
- TTL: 5 minutes

**REQ-DPM-DATA-003:** Active device connections:
- Key: `perf:active:{device_id}`
- Type: Hash (last_seen, connection_id)
- TTL: 30 seconds (refreshed on each metric)

---

## 7. API Requirements

### 7.1 REST API Endpoints

#### 7.1.1 Metrics API
```
GET /api/v1/performance/devices/{deviceId}/metrics/current
GET /api/v1/performance/devices/{deviceId}/metrics/history?start={timestamp}&end={timestamp}&type={metric_type}
GET /api/v1/performance/devices/metrics/summary?deviceIds={ids}&start={timestamp}&end={timestamp}
```

#### 7.1.2 Alerts API
```
GET /api/v1/performance/alerts?status={active|acknowledged}&severity={warning|critical}
POST /api/v1/performance/alerts/{alertId}/acknowledge
GET /api/v1/performance/alerts/thresholds
POST /api/v1/performance/alerts/thresholds
PUT /api/v1/performance/alerts/thresholds/{thresholdId}
DELETE /api/v1/performance/alerts/thresholds/{thresholdId}
```

#### 7.1.3 Reports API
```
POST /api/v1/performance/reports/generate
GET /api/v1/performance/reports
GET /api/v1/performance/reports/{reportId}
GET /api/v1/performance/reports/{reportId}/download
DELETE /api/v1/performance/reports/{reportId}
```

### 7.2 WebSocket API

#### 7.2.1 Metrics Stream
```
WS /ws/performance/metrics/{deviceId}

Message format:
{
  "type": "metric_update",
  "device_id": "uuid",
  "timestamp": "2026-05-16T13:30:00Z",
  "metrics": {
    "cpu_usage": 45.2,
    "ram_usage": 62.8,
    "battery_level": 85,
    "temperature": 38.5
  }
}
```

#### 7.2.2 Alert Stream
```
WS /ws/performance/alerts

Message format:
{
  "type": "alert",
  "alert_id": "uuid",
  "device_id": "uuid",
  "device_name": "Pixel 6",
  "metric_type": "cpu_usage",
  "severity": "warning",
  "current_value": 85.3,
  "threshold_value": 80.0,
  "timestamp": "2026-05-16T13:30:00Z"
}
```

---

## 8. Dependencies

### 8.1 External Dependencies
- ADB (Android Debug Bridge) - already available
- PostgreSQL 14+ with TimescaleDB extension (optional, for better time-series performance)
- Redis 6+
- Go 1.21+ for backend service
- React 18+ for UI components
- Chart.js or Recharts for data visualization

### 8.2 Internal Dependencies
- device-service (device connection management)
- auth-service (authentication/authorization)
- api-gateway (API routing)
- stream-service (WebSocket infrastructure)

---

## 9. Constraints and Assumptions

### 9.1 Constraints
- Must work with existing ADB infrastructure
- Cannot require root access on devices
- Must not interfere with existing device control functionality
- Limited to metrics available via ADB commands
- Desktop app only (no mobile/web version in this phase)

### 9.2 Assumptions
- Devices remain connected during monitoring sessions
- Network latency between device-service and devices is < 100ms
- PostgreSQL has sufficient storage for 90 days of metrics (estimate: 1KB per metric × 4 metrics × 1 reading/sec × 100 devices × 90 days ≈ 3.1TB)
- Users have sufficient permissions to view device metrics
- ADB commands return consistent output format across Android versions

---

## 10. Open Questions

1. **Q:** Should we support custom metric collection via user-defined ADB commands?
   **Status:** Pending product decision

2. **Q:** Do we need real-time alerting via email/SMS, or is in-app sufficient for MVP?
   **Status:** In-app sufficient for MVP, email/SMS in future phase

3. **Q:** Should historical data aggregation be configurable per user/organization?
   **Status:** Pending discussion

4. **Q:** Do we need role-based access control for viewing specific device metrics?
   **Status:** Yes, inherit from existing device permissions

5. **Q:** Should we implement anomaly detection using ML models?
   **Status:** Not in MVP, consider for future enhancement

---

## 11. Out of Scope

The following items are explicitly out of scope for this feature:
- Mobile app support for performance monitoring
- Integration with third-party monitoring tools (Datadog, New Relic, etc.)
- Predictive analytics and ML-based anomaly detection
- Custom metric plugins or extensions
- Performance monitoring for iOS devices
- Network traffic monitoring
- App-level performance profiling
- Automated remediation actions based on alerts

---

## 12. Acceptance Criteria

### 12.1 Feature Complete Criteria
- [ ] All functional requirements (REQ-DPM-001 through REQ-DPM-044) implemented
- [ ] All non-functional requirements met and verified through testing
- [ ] Integration with existing services completed and tested
- [ ] UI components implemented and responsive
- [ ] Database schema deployed and migrations tested
- [ ] API documentation completed
- [ ] Unit tests achieve 80%+ coverage
- [ ] Integration tests cover critical user flows
- [ ] Performance benchmarks meet specified targets

### 12.2 User Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] User can view real-time metrics for any connected device
- [ ] User can view historical metrics for the past 90 days
- [ ] User can configure alert thresholds and receive notifications
- [ ] User can generate and export performance reports
- [ ] User can compare performance across multiple devices
- [ ] System handles 100+ concurrent devices without degradation
- [ ] Alerts are delivered within 5 seconds of threshold breach
- [ ] UI updates within 2 seconds of metric collection

---

## 13. Revision History

| Version | Date | Author | Changes |
|---------|------|--------|---------|
| 1.0 | 2026-05-16 | Engineering Team | Initial requirements document |


</content>
</file>

input.text:
# Feature Requirements: Screen Recording & Playback

**Feature ID:** SRP-001  
**Priority:** High  
**Status:** Draft  
**Created:** 2026-05-16  
**Owner:** Engineering Team

---

## 1. Overview

### 1.1 Feature Summary
Screen recording and playback functionality for Android devices in the AUDIRA GRID device farm. Users can record device screen sessions as video files, store them with metadata, and replay recordings for debugging, testing, and documentation purposes.

### 1.2 Business Goals
- Enable test session documentation and replay for debugging
- Support evidence collection for test automation results
- Facilitate knowledge sharing and training through recorded sessions
- Provide audit trail for compliance and quality assurance
- Enable performance analysis of UI interactions

### 1.3 Success Metrics
- Recording latency < 500ms from device to storage
- Support concurrent recordings on 50+ devices
- Video quality at 720p or higher at 30 FPS
- Storage efficiency: < 50MB per minute of recording
- Playback startup time < 2 seconds
- 99.5% recording reliability (no dropped frames)

---

## 2. Functional Requirements

### 2.1 Recording Management

#### 2.1.1 Recording Initiation
**REQ-SRP-001:** Users SHALL be able to start screen recording from:
- Device detail view (one-click start button)
- Device list view (bulk action)
- Test automation workflow (programmatic API)

**REQ-SRP-002:** Recording start SHALL be confirmed with:
- Visual indicator on device screen (red recording dot)
- Notification in UI showing recording status
- Timestamp of recording start

**REQ-SRP-003:** The system SHALL support recording with configurable parameters:
- Video resolution (480p, 720p, 1080p) - default 720p
- Frame rate (15, 24, 30 FPS) - default 30 FPS
- Bitrate (2Mbps, 4Mbps, 8Mbps) - default 4Mbps
- Audio recording (on/off) - default off

**REQ-SRP-004:** Recording parameters SHALL be configurable per-device or globally.

#### 2.1.2 Recording Control
**REQ-SRP-005:** Users SHALL be able to:
- Pause recording (video paused, timestamp recorded)
- Resume recording (continues from pause point)
- Stop recording (finalizes video file)
- Cancel recording (discards unsaved video)

**REQ-SRP-006:** Recording duration limit SHALL be configurable:
- Default: 60 minutes
- Maximum: 480 minutes (8 hours)
- Automatic stop when limit reached

**REQ-SRP-007:** The system SHALL warn users when recording approaches duration limit:
- Warning at 90% of limit
- Final warning at 99% of limit

**REQ-SRP-008:** If device disconnects during recording:
- Recording SHALL pause automatically
- System SHALL attempt to resume when device reconnects
- If reconnection fails within 5 minutes, recording SHALL be finalized

#### 2.1.3 Recording Metadata
**REQ-SRP-009:** Each recording SHALL capture and store:
- Device ID and name
- Device model and Android version
- Recording start timestamp (UTC)
- Recording end timestamp (UTC)
- Total duration
- Video resolution and frame rate
- File size
- User who initiated recording
- Recording notes/description (optional)
- Tags (optional, for categorization)

**REQ-SRP-010:** The system SHALL automatically detect and store:
- App in foreground during recording
- Screen orientation changes
- Device connectivity status changes
- Performance metrics during recording (CPU, RAM, battery)

### 2.2 Video Storage and Management

#### 2.2.1 Storage Backend
**REQ-SRP-011:** Recorded videos SHALL be stored in:
- Local filesystem for development/testing (configurable path)
- S3-compatible object storage for production (AWS S3, MinIO, etc.)
- Fallback to local storage if S3 unavailable

**REQ-SRP-012:** Storage location SHALL be configurable via environment variables:
- `RECORDING_STORAGE_TYPE` (local, s3)
- `RECORDING_STORAGE_PATH` (for local)
- `S3_BUCKET`, `S3_REGION`, `S3_ACCESS_KEY`, `S3_SECRET_KEY` (for S3)

**REQ-SRP-013:** Video files SHALL be organized in storage with structure:
```
recordings/{year}/{month}/{day}/{device_id}/{recording_id}.mp4
```

**REQ-SRP-014:** The system SHALL implement automatic cleanup:
- Delete recordings older than 90 days (configurable)
- Warn users before deletion
- Provide export option before deletion

#### 2.2.2 Storage Optimization
**REQ-SRP-015:** The system SHALL support video compression:
- H.264 codec for MP4 format (default)
- H.265 codec for better compression (optional)
- Configurable bitrate for quality/size tradeoff

**REQ-SRP-016:** The system SHALL implement incremental recording:
- Record in chunks (5-minute segments)
- Allow playback of partial recordings
- Resume from last chunk if interrupted

**REQ-SRP-017:** The system SHALL support video deduplication:
- Detect identical frames and compress
- Useful for static UI screens

#### 2.2.3 Storage Quotas
**REQ-SRP-018:** The system SHALL enforce storage quotas:
- Per-user quota (configurable, default 100GB)
- Per-device quota (configurable, default 10GB)
- Organization-wide quota (configurable)

**REQ-SRP-019:** When quota is exceeded:
- New recordings SHALL be rejected with clear error message
- Users SHALL be notified to delete old recordings
- Admin dashboard SHALL show quota usage

### 2.3 Recording Playback

#### 2.3.1 Playback Interface
**REQ-SRP-020:** The playback interface SHALL provide:
- Full-screen video player
- Play/pause controls
- Seek bar with timeline
- Volume control (if audio recorded)
- Playback speed control (0.5x, 1x, 1.5x, 2x)
- Frame-by-frame navigation (forward/backward)

**REQ-SRP-021:** The player SHALL display:
- Current timestamp and total duration
- Recording metadata (device, date, user)
- Overlay with device info and metrics during playback
- Keyboard shortcuts for common actions

**REQ-SRP-022:** The system SHALL support multiple playback formats:
- MP4 (H.264/H.265)
- WebM (VP9)
- HLS streaming (for large files)

#### 2.3.2 Playback Performance
**REQ-SRP-023:** Playback SHALL start within 2 seconds of user request.

**REQ-SRP-024:** The system SHALL support:
- Streaming playback for large files (no full download required)
- Adaptive bitrate streaming based on network conditions
- Caching of recently played segments

**REQ-SRP-025:** Seeking in video SHALL respond within 1 second.

#### 2.3.3 Playback Features
**REQ-SRP-026:** Users SHALL be able to:
- Bookmark important moments in recording
- Add notes/annotations to bookmarks
- Export bookmarked segments as separate videos
- Share playback links with other users (with permission checks)

**REQ-SRP-027:** The system SHALL support synchronized playback:
- Play multiple recordings side-by-side for comparison
- Synchronized timeline across recordings
- Useful for comparing test runs on different devices

**REQ-SRP-028:** The system SHALL provide playback analytics:
- Heatmap of user interactions (taps, swipes)
- Timeline of app crashes or errors
- Performance metrics overlay (CPU, RAM, battery)

### 2.4 Recording Discovery and Management

#### 2.4.1 Recording List View
**REQ-SRP-029:** The UI SHALL display recordings with:
- Thumbnail preview (first frame of video)
- Device name and model
- Recording date and duration
- File size
- Tags and description
- User who recorded
- Last accessed date

**REQ-SRP-030:** Users SHALL be able to:
- Sort by date, duration, device, user
- Filter by device, date range, tags, user
- Search by description or tags
- Bulk select recordings

**REQ-SRP-031:** The system SHALL support pagination:
- Default 20 recordings per page
- Configurable page size (10-100)
- Lazy loading for large lists

#### 2.4.2 Recording Actions
**REQ-SRP-032:** Users SHALL be able to:
- Download recording to local machine
- Delete recording (with confirmation)
- Rename recording
- Add/edit tags and description
- Share recording link (with expiration)
- Move to archive (soft delete)

**REQ-SRP-033:** Bulk actions SHALL support:
- Delete multiple recordings
- Add tags to multiple recordings
- Download multiple recordings as ZIP
- Change retention policy for multiple recordings

#### 2.4.3 Recording Retention
**REQ-SRP-034:** The system SHALL support configurable retention policies:
- Automatic deletion after X days (default 90)
- Keep indefinitely (manual deletion only)
- Archive after X days (move to cheaper storage)

**REQ-SRP-035:** Retention policies SHALL be configurable:
- Globally (default for all recordings)
- Per-user
- Per-device
- Per-tag

### 2.5 Integration with Test Automation

#### 2.5.1 Programmatic Recording API
**REQ-SRP-036:** Test automation frameworks SHALL be able to:
- Start recording via API call
- Stop recording via API call
- Get recording ID and metadata
- Attach recording to test result

**REQ-SRP-037:** Recording API SHALL support:
```
POST /api/v1/recordings/start
{
  "device_id": "uuid",
  "resolution": "720p",
  "fps": 30,
  "tags": ["test-run", "regression"],
  "description": "Login flow test"
}

POST /api/v1/recordings/{recording_id}/stop

GET /api/v1/recordings/{recording_id}
```

**REQ-SRP-038:** Test results SHALL automatically link to recordings:
- Recording ID stored with test result
- Playback accessible from test report
- Recording metadata included in test report

#### 2.5.2 Conditional Recording
**REQ-SRP-039:** The system SHALL support conditional recording:
- Record only on test failure
- Record only for specific test cases
- Record based on device properties
- Record based on time of day

**REQ-SRP-040:** Recording triggers SHALL be configurable via:
- Test automation framework configuration
- UI settings
- API parameters

### 2.6 Video Processing and Conversion

#### 2.6.1 Post-Recording Processing
**REQ-SRP-041:** After recording stops, the system SHALL:
- Finalize video file (ensure proper MP4 headers)
- Generate thumbnail image
- Calculate video duration and file size
- Verify video integrity (playable)
- Store metadata in database

**REQ-SRP-042:** Processing SHALL be asynchronous:
- User notified when processing complete
- Recording available for playback during processing
- Processing status visible in UI

#### 2.6.2 Format Conversion
**REQ-SRP-043:** The system SHALL support on-demand format conversion:
- MP4 to WebM
- MP4 to HLS
- MP4 to GIF (short clips)
- Conversion triggered by user request

**REQ-SRP-044:** Conversion SHALL be asynchronous with:
- Progress tracking
- Estimated completion time
- Notification when complete
- Converted file stored separately

### 2.7 Permissions and Access Control

#### 2.7.1 Recording Permissions
**REQ-SRP-045:** Recording permissions SHALL be based on:
- Device access permissions (inherit from device-service)
- User role (admin, user, viewer)
- Organization membership

**REQ-SRP-046:** Permission levels:
- **Admin:** Create, view, delete, share any recording
- **User:** Create, view, delete own recordings; view shared recordings
- **Viewer:** View only; cannot create or delete

**REQ-SRP-047:** Users SHALL NOT be able to:
- View recordings they don't have permission for
- Delete recordings they don't own (unless admin)
- Share recordings without permission

#### 2.7.2 Sharing and Collaboration
**REQ-SRP-048:** Users SHALL be able to share recordings via:
- Direct link with expiration (1 day, 7 days, 30 days, never)
- Email invitation (future phase)
- Organization-wide access (admin only)

**REQ-SRP-049:** Shared links SHALL:
- Require no authentication
- Be revocable at any time
- Track access (who viewed, when)
- Support password protection (optional)

---

## 3. Non-Functional Requirements

### 3.1 Performance
**REQ-SRP-NFR-001:** Recording SHALL NOT impact device performance by more than 5% CPU usage.

**REQ-SRP-NFR-002:** The recording service SHALL support 50+ concurrent recordings.

**REQ-SRP-NFR-003:** Video encoding SHALL complete within 2x real-time (1 minute of video encoded in < 2 minutes).

**REQ-SRP-NFR-004:** Playback startup time SHALL be < 2 seconds for files up to 1GB.

**REQ-SRP-NFR-005:** Seeking in video SHALL respond within 1 second.

**REQ-SRP-NFR-006:** Storage operations (upload, download) SHALL achieve > 50Mbps throughput.

### 3.2 Scalability
**REQ-SRP-NFR-007:** The system SHALL scale to support 1000+ concurrent devices with recording capability.

**REQ-SRP-NFR-008:** Storage backend SHALL support petabyte-scale data (S3 or equivalent).

**REQ-SRP-NFR-009:** Database queries for recording metadata SHALL return results within 2 seconds for 100,000+ recordings.

### 3.3 Reliability
**REQ-SRP-NFR-010:** Recording reliability SHALL be 99.5% (no dropped frames or corruption).

**REQ-SRP-NFR-011:** Video files SHALL be verified for integrity after recording completes.

**REQ-SRP-NFR-012:** Failed recordings SHALL be automatically retried up to 3 times.

**REQ-SRP-NFR-013:** Partial recordings SHALL be recoverable if process crashes.

### 3.4 Security
**REQ-SRP-NFR-014:** All recording API endpoints SHALL require authentication via JWT tokens.

**REQ-SRP-NFR-015:** Recordings SHALL be encrypted at rest (AES-256 for S3).

**REQ-SRP-NFR-016:** Recordings in transit SHALL use HTTPS/TLS encryption.

**REQ-SRP-NFR-017:** Access to recordings SHALL be logged for audit purposes.

**REQ-SRP-NFR-018:** Sensitive data in recordings (passwords, tokens) SHALL NOT be indexed or searchable.

### 3.5 Maintainability
**REQ-SRP-NFR-019:** Recording service SHALL be implemented as a separate microservice in Go.

**REQ-SRP-NFR-020:** Codebase SHALL include unit tests with 80%+ coverage.

**REQ-SRP-NFR-021:** System SHALL expose Prometheus metrics for observability.

**REQ-SRP-NFR-022:** Configuration SHALL be externalized via environment variables.

---

## 4. Integration Requirements

### 4.1 Device Service Integration
**REQ-SRP-INT-001:** Recording service SHALL integrate with device-service via gRPC/REST API.

**REQ-SRP-INT-002:** Device-service SHALL provide:
- Device connection status
- Device metadata (model, OS version)
- ADB connection for screen recording commands

**REQ-SRP-INT-003:** Recording service SHALL use ADB command:
```
adb shell screenrecord --output-format=h264 /sdcard/recording.mp4
```

### 4.2 Stream Service Integration
**REQ-SRP-INT-004:** Recording service MAY integrate with stream-service for:
- Reusing existing WebSocket infrastructure
- Coordinating with live streaming
- Preventing conflicts between recording and streaming

### 4.3 Storage Integration
**REQ-SRP-INT-005:** Recording service SHALL support:
- Local filesystem storage (development)
- AWS S3 (production)
- MinIO (self-hosted S3-compatible)
- Azure Blob Storage (future)

**REQ-SRP-INT-006:** Storage backend SHALL be abstracted via interface for easy switching.

### 4.4 Database Integration
**REQ-SRP-INT-007:** Recording service SHALL use PostgreSQL for metadata storage.

**REQ-SRP-INT-008:** Database schema SHALL support efficient queries for:
- Recordings by device
- Recordings by user
- Recordings by date range
- Recordings by tags

### 4.5 API Gateway Integration
**REQ-SRP-INT-009:** All recording APIs SHALL be exposed through api-gateway.

**REQ-SRP-INT-010:** API-gateway SHALL implement rate limiting:
- 100 requests/minute for recording list
- 10 concurrent recordings per user
- 1GB/hour download limit per user

### 4.6 Auth Service Integration
**REQ-SRP-INT-011:** Recording service SHALL validate JWT tokens via auth-service.

**REQ-SRP-INT-012:** Permission checks SHALL be performed for all recording operations.

## 6. Data Requirements

### 6.1 Database Schema

#### 6.1.1 recordings Table
```sql
CREATE TABLE recordings (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    device_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES devices(id),
    user_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),
    file_path TEXT NOT NULL,
    file_size BIGINT NOT NULL,
    duration_seconds INTEGER NOT NULL,
    resolution VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL,
    fps INTEGER NOT NULL,
    bitrate_kbps INTEGER NOT NULL,
    codec VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL,
    has_audio BOOLEAN DEFAULT false,
    thumbnail_path TEXT,
    status VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL, -- 'recording', 'processing', 'ready', 'failed'
    error_message TEXT,
    created_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
    completed_at TIMESTAMP,
    deleted_at TIMESTAMP
);
```

#### 6.1.2 recording_metadata Table
```sql
CREATE TABLE recording_metadata (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    recording_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES recordings(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    device_model VARCHAR(255),
    android_version VARCHAR(20),
    app_in_foreground VARCHAR(255),
    description TEXT,
    tags TEXT[],
    retention_days INTEGER,
    created_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
);
```

#### 6.1.3 recording_bookmarks Table
```sql
CREATE TABLE recording_bookmarks (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    recording_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES recordings(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    user_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),
    timestamp_seconds INTEGER NOT NULL,
    note TEXT,
    created_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
);
```

#### 6.1.4 recording_shares Table
```sql
CREATE TABLE recording_shares (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    recording_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES recordings(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    shared_by UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),
    share_token VARCHAR(255) UNIQUE NOT NULL,
    expires_at TIMESTAMP,
    password_hash VARCHAR(255),
    view_count INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
    created_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
);
```

#### 6.1.5 recording_access_log Table
```sql
CREATE TABLE recording_access_log (
    id BIGSERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
    recording_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES recordings(id),
    user_id UUID REFERENCES users(id),
    share_token VARCHAR(255),
    action VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL, -- 'view', 'download', 'share'
    ip_address INET,
    user_agent TEXT,
    created_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
);
```

### 6.2 File Storage Structure
```
recordings/
├── 2026/
│   ├── 05/
│   │   ├── 16/
│   │   │   ├── device-uuid-1/
│   │   │   │   ├── recording-uuid-1.mp4
│   │   │   │   ├── recording-uuid-1.jpg (thumbnail)
│   │   │   │   └── recording-uuid-1.json (metadata)
│   │   │   └── device-uuid-2/
│   │   │       └── ...
```

## 7. API Requirements

### 7.1 REST API Endpoints

#### 7.1.1 Recording Control API
```
POST /api/v1/recordings/start
{
  "device_id": "uuid",
  "resolution": "720p",
  "fps": 30,
  "bitrate_kbps": 4000,
  "has_audio": false,
  "duration_limit_seconds": 3600,
  "tags": ["test-run"],
  "description": "Login flow test"
}
Response: { "recording_id": "uuid", "status": "recording" }

POST /api/v1/recordings/{recording_id}/stop
Response: { "recording_id": "uuid", "status": "processing" }

POST /api/v1/recordings/{recording_id}/pause
POST /api/v1/recordings/{recording_id}/resume
POST /api/v1/recordings/{recording_id}/cancel
```

#### 7.1.2 Recording Management API
```
GET /api/v1/recordings?device_id={uuid}&user_id={uuid}&tags={tag}&limit=20&offset=0
GET /api/v1/recordings/{recording_id}
PUT /api/v1/recordings/{recording_id}
{
  "description": "Updated description",
  "tags": ["new-tag"],
  "retention_days": 30
}
DELETE /api/v1/recordings/{recording_id}
GET /api/v1/recordings/{recording_id}/download
```

#### 7.1.3 Playback API
```
GET /api/v1/recordings/{recording_id}/stream?start={seconds}&end={seconds}
GET /api/v1/recordings/{recording_id}/thumbnail
GET /api/v1/recordings/{recording_id}/metadata
```

#### 7.1.4 Sharing API
```
POST /api/v1/recordings/{recording_id}/share
{
  "expires_in_days": 7,
  "password": "optional-password"
}
Response: { "share_token": "token", "share_url": "https://..." }

GET /api/v1/share/{share_token}
DELETE /api/v1/recordings/{recording_id}/share/{share_token}
```

#### 7.1.5 Bookmarks API
```
POST /api/v1/recordings/{recording_id}/bookmarks
{
  "timestamp_seconds": 120,
  "note": "App crashed here"
}

GET /api/v1/recordings/{recording_id}/bookmarks
DELETE /api/v1/recordings/{recording_id}/bookmarks/{bookmark_id}
```

## 9. Constraints and Assumptions

### 9.1 Constraints
- Recording requires ADB access to device
- Cannot record system audio without root (Android limitation)
- Video quality limited by device screen resolution
- Storage space must be pre-allocated or managed
- Recording stops if device disconnects

### 9.2 Assumptions
- Devices have sufficient storage for temporary recording files
- Network bandwidth sufficient for video upload (> 1Mbps)
- Users have storage quota configured
- ADB screenrecord command available on all Android versions 5.0+
- FFmpeg available on recording service host

---

## 10. Open Questions

1. **Q:** Should we support audio recording from device speakers?
   **Status:** Not in MVP (requires root or workarounds)

2. **Q:** Should recordings be automatically compressed after X days?
   **Status:** Pending decision on storage cost vs. quality tradeoff

3. **Q:** Do we need real-time transcoding for different formats?
   **Status:** On-demand conversion sufficient for MVP

4. **Q:** Should we support live streaming of recordings?
   **Status:** Future enhancement, not MVP

5. **Q:** Do we need ML-based object detection in recordings?
   **Status:** Future enhancement, not MVP

---

## 11. Out of Scope

The following items are explicitly out of scope for this feature:
- Audio recording from device speakers (requires root)
- Live streaming of recordings
- Real-time transcoding
- ML-based object detection or analysis
- iOS device recording
- Automatic video editing or trimming
- Integration with video hosting platforms (YouTube, Vimeo)
- Subtitle/caption generation
- Video watermarking

## 13. User Stories

### Story 1: Record Device Session
**As a** QA engineer  
**I want to** record a device screen session during test execution  
**So that** I can review the test flow and debug failures

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- Can start recording with one click
- Recording indicator visible on device
- Can stop recording and see it in recordings list
- Recording is playable immediately after stop

### Story 2: Playback and Debug
**As a** developer  
**I want to** play back a recorded session and seek to specific moments  
**So that** I can understand what happened during a test failure

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- Playback starts within 2 seconds
- Can seek to any point in video
- Can adjust playback speed
- Can bookmark important moments

### Story 3: Share Recording
**As a** QA lead  
**I want to** share a recording with team members  
**So that** they can review the test session without needing access to the system

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- Can generate shareable link
- Link expires after configured time
- Can revoke link at any time
- Shared link doesn't require authentication

### Story 4: Manage Recordings
**As a** admin  
**I want to** manage storage and retention of recordings  
**So that** I can control costs and comply with data retention policies

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- Can set retention policy (auto-delete after X days)
- Can view storage usage
- Can bulk delete old recordings
- Can archive recordings to cheaper storage
